title: Implement core test framework with layered architecture
description: Build the core server testing framework infrastructure including: base test runner, layer abstraction system, plugin registration mechanism, shared utilities, and configuration management. This forms the foundation for all subsequent layer implementations.

criteria: Framework can discover and load tests from multiple layers via pytest plugin system
Layer context can be propagated from Hardware -> Component -> BMC/BIOS hierarchy
Plugin registration via entry points successfully discovers custom test modules
Base test fixtures (server_context, hardware_info, component_map) are available to all layers
convergence.verification: Run pytest --collect-only to verify all layers are discovered; run tests/integration/test_core_integration.py for context propagation test

files.target: Test runner engine with layer orchestration
Shared utilities (logger, result aggregator, hardware probes)
Package setup with entry points for plugin registration
Configuration file for server test framework (layers, plugins, defaults)

implementation: Implement LayerContext as a dataclass holding server state, hardware info, and component map
Implement LayerProtocol defining the interface each layer must implement (setup, execute, teardown)
Build TestRunnerEngine class that orchestrates multi-layer test execution
Create PluginRegistry using importlib.metadata entry_points for dynamic test module discovery
Define shared pytest fixtures: server_context (server metadata), hardware_info (CPU, memory, storage), component_map (enumerated components)
Implement Config class loading from server-test.yaml with environment variable overrides
Create utilities: get_logger (logging), aggregate_results (test result collection), probe_hardware (hardware enumeration)
Write unit tests covering layer abstraction, plugin discovery, and fixture behavior
Write integration test verifying cross-layer context propagation
Configure setup.py with pytest plugin entry point and layer-specific console scripts
